[[Category:grammatical numbers]]
The '''plural''' in [[Aelaki]] is a grammatical number with partitive meaning, indicating "some of" a group rather than all of it. For detailed formation rules and examples, see '''[[Aelaki plural]]'''.

The plural is one of six grammatical numbers in Aelaki, alongside the [[Singular|singular]], [[Paucal|paucal]], [[Plentiful|plentiful]], [[Collective|collective]], and [[Zero|zero]].

== Overview ==

The plural is formed by '''reduplication''' of the second root consonant (C₂), producing the template C₁V₁C₂C₂V₂C₃. Unlike the [[Collective|collective]], which indicates all members of a group, the plural carries a partitive sense: "some of" the referent.

{| class="wikitable"
! Singular !! Plural !! Gloss
|-
| Basol (tree) || Basosol || trees (some of them)
|}

== Historical Development ==

The plural reduplication originally developed from frequentative verb reduplication. Over time, this verbal pattern merged with subject-verb agreement marking and became generalized as the nominal plural.

== See Also ==

* [[Aelaki plural]] -- detailed article
* [[Singular]]
* [[Paucal]]
* [[Plentiful]]
* [[Collective]]
* [[Zero]]

[[Category:git synced pages]]
